## SECTION 1 — Global Pulse (Geopolitics & World Economy)
Crude oil prices have declined as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz largely normalized, easing supply concerns. This follows an initial agreement reached to end the US-Israeli conflict with Iran, allowing a 60-day negotiation period for broader issues. However, a reported drone attack on a Singapore-flagged container ship in the Strait of Hormuz has since rekindled fears of renewed disruption in the vital waterway. Globally, inflation rates have risen sharply in 2026, largely attributed to the war in Iran and the ongoing closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

## SECTION 2 — Global News (Complete Continental Coverage)
### Americas
TTM Technologies, Inc. is expected to join the Russell 1000 Index, a key benchmark for large-cap U.S. stocks, after the market close on June 26, 2026. The 2026 FIFA World Cup, featuring 48 national teams, commenced on June 11 and continues until July 19, with matches hosted in Canada, Mexico, and the United States. Hyperscale Data has rescheduled its investor conference call to June 26, 2026. The U.S. Army announced the establishment of a new Space Operations Branch, effective October 1, 2026, creating a permanent career path in space operations for military personnel.

### Europe
A doctor in France recently tested positive for Ebola after returning from a humanitarian mission in the Democratic Republic of Congo, marking Europe's first confirmed case linked to the ongoing outbreak. The World Health Organization (WHO) Chief has stated that the global risk from this Ebola outbreak remains low. The ICRA 2026, a major conference on robotics and automation, is taking place in Vienna from June 1-5.

### Asia-Pacific
NASA has selected Rocket Lab to provide three Electron launches for two separate missions, PolSIR and TSIS-2, starting early next year from New Zealand. These missions will focus on studying ice clouds at high altitudes and the Sun-Earth energy balance. MWC Shanghai, with a focus on AI, robotics, and drones, opened on June 24 and is scheduled to run until June 27, featuring humanoid robots and a football penalties challenge.

### Africa
The United Nations has allocated $8 million in funding to assist Burundi and South Sudan in preparing for the potential spread of Ebola, following an outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. UNICEF has expressed condolences after five children were killed and seven others injured by an explosive ordnance in the village of Raybi in Yemen. Madagascar celebrates its National Day on June 26, commemorating its independence from France.

## SECTION 6 — Science & Research Breakthroughs
A global effort is underway to gain recognition for toxoplasmosis as a neglected tropical disease (NTD) by the World Health Organization. This parasite, which infects one-third of the global population, is a leading cause of ocular toxoplasmosis, an eye infection that can result in permanent vision loss. Scientists emphasize that WHO recognition could lead to significant advancements in the prevention and management of this infection. NASA's upcoming PolSIR and TSIS-2 missions, scheduled for early 2027, are designed to gather critical data on high-altitude ice clouds and the Sun's energy input to Earth, respectively.

Crude oil prices have declined as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz largely normalized following an initial peace agreement between the U.S., Israel, and Iran. ## SECTION 13 — India National (Politics & Governance)
The Central government has advised farmers in rain-fed regions with limited irrigation to switch to less water-intensive crops such as pulses, oilseeds, and millets, as monsoon rains have been 42% below average since June 1. India and China show signs of improving relations, with National Security Adviser Ajit Doval and Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi holding constructive and forward-looking talks on the sidelines of a BRICS security meeting. An FIR has been registered in Ayodhya over alleged donation embezzlement at the Ram Temple, with authorities initiating an investigation. The government clarified that a passport has never been proof of citizenship, citing the Passports Act and the Bombay High Court, amidst a political row. Amazon CEO met with Prime Minister Modi and announced an investment of $48 billion in India from 2026-2030. NCERT has introduced a section on the Emergency in the Class IX textbook for the first time. Prime Minister Narendra Modi asserted that the Indian economy remains exceptionally strong despite the global economic crisis. The Congress party has launched a nationwide campaign focusing on exam reforms, highlighting paper leaks, examination irregularities, and unemployment. Global firms and investors have committed over $90 billion in new investments to India. Oil prices have fallen, but fuel rates in India remain unchanged.

## SECTION 14 — Telangana State Updates
Hyderabad Traffic Police have announced extensive traffic restrictions and diversions in the Old City from 12 noon to 10 p.m. today, June 26, for the Bibi-ka-Alam procession on the 10th day of Muharram. Schools, colleges, and government offices in Hyderabad are closed today, June 26, due to a holiday declared for the 10th Muharram (Yaum-e-Ashura). The Telangana government has offered three alternate prime sites to the State Bank of India (SBI) to resolve a long-standing dispute over a land parcel in Raidurg. BRS Working President KT Rama Rao (KTR) criticized the Congress government, alleging it "deceived every section of Telangana society" by failing to implement pre-election promises and pushing the real estate sector into crisis. Property registration services in Telangana are unavailable for three days starting June 26, due to server maintenance by the registration and stamps department, with June 26 being a government holiday for Muharram. Telangana CM A Revanth Reddy attended a meeting with Karnataka CM DK Shivakumar, Andhra Pradesh CM N Chandrababu Naidu, and Union Jal Shakti minister CR Patil regarding water-sharing disputes, specifically around the Tungabhadra Dam, aiming for a lasting solution.

## SECTION 15 — Andhra Pradesh State Updates
Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu has renewed his call for interlinking of rivers, emphasizing the need for intra-linking within states first, and then interlinking between states, advocating for a national approach to water management. The "Rottela Panduga" (Festival of Rotis) annual festival is set to begin today, June 26, in Nellore, with approximately 14 lakh people expected to attend over four days. The Andhra Pradesh government has revised the holiday schedule for Muharram 2026, declaring today, June 26, as a General Holiday on account of 10th Muharram (Ashura), changing it from the earlier scheduled June 25. Minister of Municipal Administration and Urban Development P. Narayana stated that Chief Minister Nara Chandrababu allocated ₹5 crore for the development of Bara Shaheed Dargah, alleging neglect by the previous YSR Congress Party government. Andhra Pradesh will upgrade 16 de-addiction centers with a ₹10 crore fund. A PIL (Public Interest Litigation) in service matters is not allowed, as per the Andhra Pradesh High Court. The Andhra Pradesh High Court ruled that a six-month cooling-off period is not mandatory in divorce cases if other conditions are met. Temples in Andhra Pradesh earned ₹22.26 crore via the Mana Mitra WhatsApp platform.

## SECTION 18 — Deep Analysis of Most Important Breaking News
**What Happened:** Crude oil prices experienced fluctuations due to conflicting developments concerning the Strait of Hormuz. Initial declines were observed as tanker traffic through the strategic waterway normalized following an agreement between the U.S., Israel, and Iran. However, a reported drone attack on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz subsequently rekindled fears of renewed disruptions and caused prices to climb.
**Root Causes:** The initial softening of oil prices was driven by easing geopolitical tensions and improved global oil flow through the Strait of Hormuz, a result of an initial peace agreement. The subsequent price volatility and renewed concerns stem directly from the reported drone strike, indicating persistent instability and the potential for interruptions in this critical global oil transit route.
**Historical Background:** The region has been affected by the "2026 Iran war," which previously caused significant spikes in oil prices. The Strait of Hormuz is recognized as a historically sensitive choke point, crucial for the passage of a substantial portion of the world's oil supply, and has been a focal point of geopolitical tensions for decades.
**Key Actors:** Key actors include the United States, Israel, Iran, and global oil markets, along with international shipping companies that rely on the Strait of Hormuz.
**Strategic Importance:** The Strait of Hormuz is strategically vital for global energy security, with over 20 million barrels of oil reportedly transiting the strait in the past 24 hours. Any disruptions in this waterway have immediate and significant impacts on global crude oil prices and the wider world economy.
**Possible Long-Term Consequences:** Continued instability and potential for further attacks in the Strait of Hormuz could lead to sustained higher oil prices globally, contributing to inflation and economic uncertainty. Conversely, successful de-escalation and long-term security guarantees could stabilize energy markets. The incident underscores the fragility of ongoing peace processes in the region.

## SECTION 19 — This Day in History (Global)
*   1963 – U.S. President John F. Kennedy delivered his iconic "Ich bin ein Berliner" speech in West Berlin, expressing solidarity with the city's residents.
*   1945 – The United Nations Charter was signed by 50 nations in San Francisco, establishing the foundational document of the international organization.
*   1936 – The world's first practical helicopter, the twin-rotor Focke-Wulf Fw 61, achieved its initial lift-off in Bremen, Germany.
*   1977 – Elvis Presley performed his last public concert in Indianapolis.
*   1979 – Legendary heavyweight boxer Muhammad Ali officially retired from boxing.
*   1997 – J.K. Rowling's first book in the series, "Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone," was initially published.
*   1819 – The bicycle was invented.
*   1906 – The first Grand Prix car race in history was held on a circuit around Le Mans, France.
*   1960 – Madagascar gained its independence from France.
*   1974 – The Universal Product Code (UPC) was scanned for the first time on a pack of chewing gum in Troy, Ohio, marking a milestone in retail technology.

---

## SECTION 20 — This Day in History (India)
*   1975 – Prime Minister Indira Gandhi declared a state of Emergency across India, a period characterized by the suspension of civil liberties and widespread arrests.
*   1992 – The Indian army launched Operation Blue Star to remove armed militants from the Golden Temple in Amritsar, an operation with profound political and social impacts.
*   1945 – India, while still under British rule, was among the founding members of the United Nations Charter, which was signed in San Francisco.
*   1963 – Dr. Sam Manekshaw was promoted to the rank of Major General; he later became a Field Marshal and played a crucial role in the Indo-Pakistani War of 1971.
*   1947 – The Constituent Assembly of India continued its work on drafting the Constitution of India.
*   2017 – The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council finalized the GST rates for various products, a major reform to India's indirect tax system that came into effect on July 1, 2017.
*   1932 – The Poona Pact was signed between Mahatma Gandhi and Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, ensuring reserved seats for the depressed classes in provincial legislatures.
*   1914 – The Indian Relief Act was passed, which abolished a tax imposed on Indians who had not renewed their indentures and recognized the validity of Indian customary marriages.
*   1888 – Ghanshyam Das Birla, a prominent freedom fighter and social reformer, was born in Pilani.
*   1874 – Rajarshi Shahu Chhatrapati, a great revolutionary freedom fighter and social reformer, was born.

## SECTION 25 — Strategic Insight / Underreported Development
The global push to recognize toxoplasmosis as a neglected tropical disease (NTD) by the World Health Organization, despite its widespread impact, represents a significant but underreported global health challenge. Infecting one-third of the world's population and being a leading cause of permanent vision loss, the lack of sufficient attention hinders comprehensive prevention and management strategies. Elevated recognition and concerted international efforts could lead to substantial advancements in addressing this pervasive parasitic infection.
